steps.title: Count the doublings
steps.content:
From $1640$ to $1960$ is
$$
1960-1640=320
$$
years. Since the population doubled every $80$ years, the number of doublings was
$$
\frac{320}{80}=4
$$

steps.title: Work backward from 1960
steps.content:
Four doublings means the $1960$ population was $2^4=16$ times the $1640$ population. Divide by $16$:
$$
\frac{192{,}000}{16}=12{,}000
$$
